title: skopeo copy should log progress to stderr, not stdout
body:
This shows progress is printed to stdout.

```
root@st-dev-0:/# skopeo copy docker://busybox:musl oci:a 2>/dev/null
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ccf6f19e0aad done   |
Copying config 2ba859eb00 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination

root@st-dev-0:/# skopeo copy docker://busybox:musl oci:b >/dev/null

root@st-dev-0:/#
```

These "copying" and "writing" messages are not program result, but rather logs, so they should go to stderr.

The result (to stdout) could be something like JSON with statistics or one line with image id or something like that.

```
root@st-dev-0:/# skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.13.3
```

comments.body:
Code in the `image` library:

https://github.com/containers/image/blob/bc6b35d0c9d25aa14d1f0c9eeb058ae22e77a7ca/copy/single.go#L603

```
	ic.c.Printf("Writing manifest to image destination\n")
```

```
func (c *copier) Printf(format string, a ...any) {
	fmt.Fprintf(c.reportWriter, format, a...)
}
```

```
type copier struct {
        // ...
	reportWriter   io.Writer
	progressOutput io.Writer
```

Podman seems to initialize report writer with stderr as expected:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d7bc7b7b410fd70052073103e6b9e367e28af8ba/pkg/machine/ocipull/pull.go#L90

But skopeo with stdout:

https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/49569bcf693599cce3b198bfcca57f5251f60d6e/cmd/skopeo/copy.go#L296

Thanks for your report.

Using some other output destination might have been a good idea when this was originally introduced, but after so many years of stable behavior, and with Skopeo probably frequently used in scripts, changing that and risking breaking scripts just isn’t worth it.

> The result (to stdout) could be something like JSON with statistics or one line with image id or something like that.

I don’t know that there’s significant benefit to things like that (and there already is `--digestfile`), especially when used interactively on a terminal; but if we _did_ add that, it would ~certainly have to be a new option (so that script users who _rely_ on that output being present can cleanly fail when running older Skopeo) — and that option might also cause the progress bars to be drawn to some other destination.

(For reference, compare #658 .)
We aren’t going to just incompatibly change the routing. Please file other desired features separately.
